title: Automated Optical Inspection (AOI) for PCB / manufacturing defects
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence template (fan-out artifact). Physics below is grounded in canonical NDT/NDE literature for this principle.

E.description: Automated Optical Inspection (AOI) for PCB / manufacturing defects: multi angle vision produces the measurement through a 4-node primitive DAG L.illuminate_multi_angle -> K.psf.airy -> D.image_sensor -> int.spatial, with spatially-projected accumulation and additive Gaussian thermal/electronic noise. Recovery is posed as a linear inverse problem that inverts the forward operator to estimate the scene-side 2D defect mask. Difficulty tier delta=1 with effective condition number kappa_eff~5; calibration-level mismatch (illumination_variation, color_shift, part_misalignment) sets the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form imaging equation.
E.forward_model: y_k(r) = lighting_k * reflectance(r) * shading(r, pose) + n; detect defects via template matching or DL
E.world_state_x: Defect / strain / composition map depending on principle

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 2D defect mask is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by the declared priors. Stability is well-conditioned (kappa_eff ~= 5); illumination_variation dominates the stability cliff; color_shift and the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Additive gaussian thermal/electronic noise sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor, while mild Tikhonov or analytic inversion is sufficient at the nominal Omega point.

C.solver_class: analytic / closed-form + post-processing [Template-Match] | linear-operator + convex optimisation [Classic-CV] | linear-operator + deep neural prior [AOI-UNet]
C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: ||x_hat - x||_2 <= C1 * sigma_noise / sqrt(N_measurements). Mismatch-induced bias adds + C2 * |illumination_variation| + C3 * ||x - x_s||_1 (best s-sparse / low-rank approximation residual). Iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the MLE; epsilon_fn captures the monotone quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(H * W * log(...)) per iteration; learned variants: O(H W Z * F_theta_cost) per forward pass
